title: The Magic Raincoat
key_takeaway: Understanding that your true self is eternal and cannot be hurt.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Magic Raincoat — Understanding that your true self is eternal and cannot be hurt.
sanskrit:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि

न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।

अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो

न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ।।
transliteration:
na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin
nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ
ajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yaṁ purāṇo
na hanyate hanyamāne śharīre
meaning: It is never born; it never dies; having come into existence once, it never ceases to exist. Unborn, eternal, perpetual, and primordial, it is not destroyed when the body is destroyed.
content: When you look into the Mirror, you don’t just see your body—you see something deeper. Your true self never ends. It is calm, fearless, and always shining, no matter what happens outside.
story: Just like you wear a raincoat to stay dry but YOU are still under it, your body is like a coat for your soul. You are eternal and amazing!
lifeHack: Just like you wear a raincoat to stay dry but YOU are still under it, your body is like a coat for your soul. You are eternal and amazing!
power: I am fearless because my true self is eternal.

title: The Million Sparks
key_takeaway: Realizing you are a precious part of a much larger, divine light.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Million Sparks — Realizing you are a precious part of a much larger, divine light.
sanskrit: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ।।
transliteration:
mamaivānśho jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ
manaḥ-ṣhaṣhṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛiti-sthāni karṣhati
meaning: An eternal portion of Myself having become a living soul in the world of life, draws to itself the five senses, with the mind as the sixth, abiding in Nature.
content: The Mirror shows that you are not ordinary—you carry a piece of the Divine within you. Your worth is not defined by the world, but by the light inside you.
story: A giant bonfire has a million tiny sparks. You are one of those sparks—a part of the big, beautiful light of God!
lifeHack: A giant bonfire has a million tiny sparks. You are one of those sparks—a part of the big, beautiful light of God!
power: I am valuable because I carry divine light.

title: The Sandcastle
key_takeaway: Focusing on the joy of doing rather than the stress of winning.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Sandcastle — Focusing on the joy of doing rather than the stress of winning.
sanskrit:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।

मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ।।
transliteration:
karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchana
mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi
meaning: Your right is only to work, but not to its results; do not let the results of action be your motive, nor let your attachment be to inaction.
content: The Mirror reminds you to act without fear of results. When you give your best, the reflection becomes clear and strong.
story: Build a castle for the fun of it! If a wave comes, don't be sad. The joy was in the playing, not just the castle.
lifeHack: Build a castle for the fun of it! If a wave comes, don't be sad. The joy was in the playing, not just the castle.
power: I focus on effort, not outcome.

title: The Garden of the Heart
key_takeaway: Learning that you have the power to choose your own thoughts.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Garden of the Heart — Learning that you have the power to choose your own thoughts.
sanskrit:
उद्धरेदात्मनाऽऽत्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्।

आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ।।
transliteration:
uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet
ātmaiva hyātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ
meaning: One should raise oneself by one's own self alone; let not one lower oneself; for the self alone is one's own friend, and the self alone is one's own enemy.
content: Your mind is like a mirror—it reflects whatever you focus on. If you guide it, it becomes your strongest ally. If you ignore it, it creates confusion and doubt.
story: You are the gardener of your own heart. Choose to plant happy thoughts and you'll be your own best friend!
lifeHack: You are the gardener of your own heart. Choose to plant happy thoughts and you'll be your own best friend!
power: I take responsibility for my thoughts.

title: The Super Friend
key_takeaway: Finding peace by knowing you have a divine friend always helping you.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Super Friend — Finding peace by knowing you have a divine friend always helping you.
sanskrit:
भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम्।

सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ।।
transliteration:
bhoktāraṁ yajña-tapasāṁ sarva-loka-maheśhvaram
suhṛidaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ jñātvā māṁ śhāntim ṛichchhati
meaning: He who knows Me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great Lord of all the worlds, and the friend of all beings, attains peace.
content: You don’t have to carry everything alone. When you trust a higher force, your mind becomes calm and your mirror becomes still.
story: Krishna is like a super-strong friend who is always watching over you, making sure you are safe and happy.
lifeHack: Krishna is like a super-strong friend who is always watching over you, making sure you are safe and happy.
power: I release the burden and feel peace.

title: The Busy Bee Leader
key_takeaway: Recognizing that your good actions inspire others to be good too.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Busy Bee Leader — Recognizing that your good actions inspire others to be good too.
sanskrit:
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः।

स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ।।
transliteration:
yad yad ācharati śhreṣhṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ
sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate
meaning: Whatever a great man does, others do too. Whatever standard he sets, the world follows it.
content: Your actions are reflections others see. Even without knowing it, you inspire people through how you live.
story: When the Queen Bee works hard and is kind, all the other bees learn to be kind too. You can be a leader too!
lifeHack: When the Queen Bee works hard and is kind, all the other bees learn to be kind too. You can be a leader too!

title: The Big Umbrella
key_takeaway: The promise that the universe protects those who stay focused on goodness.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Big Umbrella — The promise that the universe protects those who stay focused on goodness.
sanskrit:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते।

तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ।।
transliteration:
ananyāśh chintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate
teṣhāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣhemaṁ vahāmyaham
meaning: Those who, excluding all else, think of Me and worship Me, aspiring after eternal union with Me, I look after their prosperity and welfare (Yoga and Ksema).
content: When you stay connected with faith, life supports you in unseen ways.
story: Imagine a giant umbrella of love that follows you everywhere, keeping you safe from any storm.
lifeHack: Imagine a giant umbrella of love that follows you everywhere, keeping you safe from any storm.
power: I am supported by the universe.

title: The Tiny Leaf Gift
key_takeaway: Understanding that the intention of love matters more than the size of the gift.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Tiny Leaf Gift — Understanding that the intention of love matters more than the size of the gift.
sanskrit:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति।

तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ।।
transliteration:
patraṁ puṣhpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayachchhati
tadahaṁ bhaktyupahṛitam aśhnāmi prayatātmanaḥ
meaning: Whoever offers Me with true devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or some water, I accept this offering made with devotion by them who are pure of heart.
content: Even the smallest act of kindness shines brightly in the Mirror. Love is what truly matters.
story: A little squirrel brought a single leaf with lots of love. It was the best gift ever because it came from the heart!
lifeHack: A little squirrel brought a single leaf with lots of love. It was the best gift ever because it came from the heart!

title: The Light Guard
key_takeaway: Maintaining hope that goodness will always return to the world.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Light Guard — Maintaining hope that goodness will always return to the world.
sanskrit:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।

अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदाऽऽत्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ।।
transliteration:
yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛijāmyaham
meaning: Whenever spirituality decays and materialism is rampant, then, O Arjuna, I reincarnate myself.
content: Whenever life feels chaotic, remember that balance will return. The Mirror always realigns.
story: Whenever the world feels a bit dark, a special Hero of Light appears to make everything bright and happy again.
lifeHack: Whenever the world feels a bit dark, a special Hero of Light appears to make everything bright and happy again.

title: The Season Clock
key_takeaway: Gaining perspective by understanding the vastness of time.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Season Clock — Gaining perspective by understanding the vastness of time.
sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो

लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः।

ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे

येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः ।।
transliteration:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
kālo ’smi loka-kṣhaya-kṛit pravṛiddho
lokān samāhartum iha pravṛittaḥ
ṛite ’pi tvāṁ na bhaviṣhyanti sarve
ye ’vasthitāḥ pratyanīkeṣhu yodhāḥ
meaning: The Blessed Lord said, "I am the world-destroying Time, grown in stature and now engaged in annihilating the creatures. Even without you, all the warriors arrayed in the confronting armies will cease to exist!"
content: Time reminds us that everything is temporary. Your struggles are just moments in a bigger journey.
story: Time is like a giant clock that changes the seasons. God is the one who keeps the clock ticking rhythmically.
lifeHack: Time is like a giant clock that changes the seasons. God is the one who keeps the clock ticking rhythmically.

title: The Trust Fall
key_takeaway: Letting go of guilt and fear by surrendering to divine love.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Trust Fall — Letting go of guilt and fear by surrendering to divine love.
sanskrit: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ।।
transliteration:
sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śharaṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ
meaning: Abandon all attributes and come to Me as your only refuge; I will rescue you from all sins; do not be sorrowful.
content: Release fear, guilt, and control. When you surrender, your inner mirror becomes light and free.
story: Close your eyes and fall back—God's arms are always there to catch you and keep you safe from any worry.
lifeHack: Close your eyes and fall back—God's arms are always there to catch you and keep you safe from any worry.
power: I let go and trust life.

title: The Power of Choice
key_takeaway: Empowering yourself to make your own life choices based on wisdom.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Power of Choice — Empowering yourself to make your own life choices based on wisdom.
sanskrit: विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ।।
transliteration: vimṛiśhyaitad aśheṣheṇa yathechchhasi tathā kuru
meaning: Reflect on this fully, and then do as you wish.
content: After understanding everything, the final choice is always yours. You shape your reflection through your decisions.
story: Imagine a path with many turns. God gives you a map and light, but YOU are the one who chooses which way to walk!
lifeHack: Imagine a path with many turns. God gives you a map and light, but YOU are the one who chooses which way to walk!
power: I choose my path with awareness.

title: The Winning Team
key_takeaway: The winning formula: pairing divine wisdom with human effort.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Winning Team — The winning formula: pairing divine wisdom with human effort.
sanskrit:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः।

तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम।। ।।
transliteration:
yatra yogeśhvaraḥ kṛiṣhṇो yatra pārtho dhanur-dharaḥ
tatra śhrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama
meaning: Where Krishna, the Lord of Yogis, remains, where the son of Prtha holds his bow, there lies fortune, victory, prosperity, and firm justice—so I believe.
content: When inner wisdom guides your actions, success naturally follows. This is the ultimate reflection of a balanced life.
story: When Wisdom (Krishna) and Courage (Arjuna) play on the same team, they always win the game of Life!
lifeHack: When Wisdom (Krishna) and Courage (Arjuna) play on the same team, they always win the game of Life!
power: I act with clarity and courage.

title: The Wisdom Seeker
key_takeaway: Finding a good teacher and asking questions with respect helps you learn the best.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Wisdom Seeker — Finding a good teacher and asking questions with respect helps you learn the best.
sanskrit:
तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया ।
उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥
transliteration:
tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśhnena sevayā
upadekṣhyanti te jñānaṁ jñāninas tattva-darśhinaḥ
meaning: Learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him.
story: If you want to learn how to play a new sport, you find a great coach, listen to them, and practice hard. Finding a good teacher is the secret to getting smart!
lifeHack: If you want to learn how to play a new sport, you find a great coach, listen to them, and practice hard. Finding a good teacher is the secret to getting smart!
power: I am a humble and curious learner.

title: Feeling With Others
key_takeaway: True yogis feel the joy and sadness of others as if it were their own.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: Feeling With Others — True yogis feel the joy and sadness of others as if it were their own.
sanskrit:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन ।
सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमो मतः ॥
transliteration:
ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṁ paśhyati yo 'rjuna
sukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ sa yogī paramo mataḥ
meaning: He who judges of pleasure or pain everywhere by the same standard as he applies to himself, that yogi, O Arjuna, is regarded as the highest.
story: When your friend wins a game, you cheer for them. When they scrape their knee, you help them up. Feeling what others feel makes your heart super strong!
lifeHack: When your friend wins a game, you cheer for them. When they scrape their knee, you help them up. Feeling what others feel makes your heart super strong!
power: I understand and care for others.

title: Skill in Everything
key_takeaway: Yoga is not just stretching; it is doing whatever you do with all your heart and skill.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: Skill in Everything — Yoga is not just stretching; it is doing whatever you do with all your heart and skill.
sanskrit:
बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते ।
तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥
transliteration:
buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛita-duṣhkṛite
tasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśhalam
meaning: A man engaged in devotional service rids himself of both good and bad actions even in this life. Therefore strive for yoga, which is the art of all work.
story: Whether you are doing math homework or painting a picture, doing it with full focus and giving it your best try is actually a type of Yoga!
lifeHack: Whether you are doing math homework or painting a picture, doing it with full focus and giving it your best try is actually a type of Yoga!
power: I do my very best in everything.

title: The Steady Mind
key_takeaway: A steady-minded person finds happiness inside themselves, no matter what happens outside.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: The Steady Mind — A steady-minded person finds happiness inside themselves, no matter what happens outside.
sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच
प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान् ।
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥
transliteration:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatān
ātmany evātmanā tuṣhṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadochyate
meaning: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: O Arjuna, when a man gives up all varieties of desire for sense gratification and finds satisfaction in the self alone, he is said to be in pure transcendental consciousness.
story: Imagine a deep ocean. Even when waves crash on top, deep down it is calm and quiet. You can find that same quiet place inside yourself whenever you need it.
lifeHack: Imagine a deep ocean. Even when waves crash on top, deep down it is calm and quiet. You can find that same quiet place inside yourself whenever you need it.

title: God Loves Everyone
key_takeaway: God doesn't have favorites; the Divine loves everyone equally.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: God Loves Everyone — God doesn't have favorites; the Divine loves everyone equally.
sanskrit:
समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः ।
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
transliteration:
samo 'haṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu na me dveṣhyo 'sti na priyaḥ
ye bhajanti tu māṁ bhaktyā mayi te teṣhu chāpy aham
meaning: I envy no one, nor am I partial to anyone. I am equal to all. But whoever renders service unto Me in devotion is a friend, is in Me, and I am also a friend to him.
story: Just like the sun shines its warm light on every tree, flower, and person without picking favorites, God's love shines on everyone equally.
lifeHack: Just like the sun shines its warm light on every tree, flower, and person without picking favorites, God's love shines on everyone equally.
power: I treat everyone with equal love.

title: Work is Worship
key_takeaway: Doing your chores or homework with a happy heart is a way of saying 'thank you' to the universe.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita wisdom for children: Work is Worship — Doing your chores or homework with a happy heart is a way of saying 'thank you' to the universe.
sanskrit:
यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।
स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ॥
transliteration:
yataḥ pravṛittir bhūtānāṁ yena sarvam idaṁ tatam
sva-karmaṇā tam abhyarchya siddhiṁ vindati mānavaḥ
meaning: By worship of the Lord, who is the source of all beings and who is all-pervading, a man can attain perfection through performing his own work.
story: You don't just have to pray in a temple. When you help your parents clean or focus hard on a test, you are turning your work into a beautiful prayer!
lifeHack: You don't just have to pray in a temple. When you help your parents clean or focus hard on a test, you are turning your work into a beautiful prayer!
power: My work is my way of giving thanks.

title: Controlling the Mind
key_takeaway: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 6.6: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends.
sanskrit: // Sanskrit for 6.6 pending
transliteration: // Transliteration for 6.6 pending
meaning: // Meaning for 6.6 pending
lifeHack: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends.
